stories.title: The Navy’s Strategy to Weaponize Data and Artificial Intelligence
stories.simple_summary: The U.S. Navy is planning to use artificial intelligence and data more strategically for military purposes.
stories.why_it_matters: This is about military strategy and doesn't affect ordinary people or small businesses — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Editorial: AI in the workplace
stories.simple_summary: A news outlet is discussing how AI is changing workplaces and what that means for workers.
stories.why_it_matters: Depending on what they said, this could be relevant to small business owners thinking about using AI tools, but without seeing the full article it's hard to say specifically what applies to you.

stories.title: Apple Intelligence approved for launch in China with Alibaba and Baidu
stories.simple_summary: Apple is allowing two Chinese companies (Alibaba and Baidu) to help run Apple's AI features in China instead of running them directly.
stories.why_it_matters: If you don't use Apple products in China, this doesn't affect you — this is mostly about Apple doing business in a specific country.

stories.title: Claude can now use your 1Password credentials for you
stories.simple_summary: Claude, an AI chatbot, can now ask your permission to log into your accounts (like travel booking websites) and do tasks for you using your stored passwords.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Claude and trust it with sensitive tasks, this could save time, but it means trusting an AI tool with your login information — think carefully before allowing this.

stories.title: Behind the risks and benefits of letting AI manage your money
stories.simple_summary: An article discusses the good and bad sides of letting AI software handle your investments and money decisions.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're considering AI tools to manage your savings or investments, you'll want to understand the risks and benefits before trusting your money to them.

stories.title: The Quest for ‘Technological Sovereignty’ in Europe (and Why It’s So Hard)
stories.simple_summary: Europe is trying to build its own AI technology so it doesn't rely completely on American companies, but it's proving very difficult.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly about government and business competition — it doesn't directly affect your daily life or small business.

stories.title: Google ordered to open Android and Search to rivals in Europe
stories.simple_summary: The European Union ordered Google to let other companies' AI assistants and search engines work better with Android phones and Google Search.
stories.why_it_matters: This might eventually give you more choices in search engines and AI helpers on your phone, but it's mainly a legal decision affecting big tech companies.

stories.title: Computer cops
stories.simple_summary: A news story is exploring how police departments are using AI technology in their work.
stories.why_it_matters: Depending on details, this could be relevant to how law enforcement operates in your area, but without more information it's hard to say what it means for you.

stories.title: AI chatbots are at risk of spreading government restrictions on online speech, a new study says
stories.simple_summary: A study warns that AI chatbots might repeat and spread government censorship and speech restrictions from around the world.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly a concern for researchers and policymakers — it doesn't immediately change how you can use AI tools today.

stories.title: Nobel Laureates sign Rome Declaration on Nuclear Weapons and AI
stories.simple_summary: Nobel Prize winners signed a declaration expressing concern about the risks of nuclear weapons combined with artificial intelligence.
stories.why_it_matters: This is about serious global issues but doesn't require any action from ordinary people or small business owners.

stories.title: For Software Engineers, the AI Reckoning Is Already Here
stories.simple_summary: Software engineers who write computer code are experiencing big changes in their jobs because of AI tools that can write code.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're not a software engineer, this doesn't directly affect you — it's mostly relevant to people in tech jobs.

stories.title: Applied Computing wants to give oil and gas operators an AI model for the entire plant
stories.simple_summary: A company raised $20 million to build an AI system that can manage and optimize entire oil and gas plants.
stories.why_it_matters: Unless you work in the oil, gas, or petrochemical industry, this doesn't affect you — it's a specialized tool for large industrial operations.
